{"parts":[{"tool_name":"read_file","content":["# Frontend Template Schema Specification
# This file defines the YAML schema for frontend templates.
# Use this as a reference when creating new templates.

---
# TEMPLATE METADATA
# Basic information about the template

name: template-slug  # REQUIRED: lowercase, hyphens only (e.g., "clean-saas", "bold-agency")
description: |  # REQUIRED: Multi-line description
  What this template is good for and when to use it.
  Be specific about the target audience, use case, and aesthetic.
  Examples:
  - "Clean, professional SaaS landing pages with subtle shadows and ample whitespace"
  - "Bold, vibrant agency portfolio with large typography and dynamic layouts"

source: |  # REQUIRED: Attribution and context
  URL of the original page this template was derived from, or description of inspiration.
  Examples:
  - "https://example.com/inspiration-page"
  - "Inspired by modern fintech apps, synthesized from Stripe, Plaid, and Mercury"

created: YYYY-MM-DD  # REQUIRED: Creation date in ISO format

---
# COLOR PALETTE
# All colors are REQUIRED. Use hex values (#000000 format).
# This palette should work harmoniously across all sections.

palette:
  primary: "#hex"      # Main brand color - used for primary CTAs, headers, key elements
  secondary: "#hex"    # Supporting color - used for secondary elements, subtle highlights  
  accent: "#hex"       # Accent color - used for highlights, CTAs, interactive elements
  background: "#hex"   # Page background - usually white (#ffffff) or dark (#0a0a0a)
  surface: "#hex"      # Card/section backgrounds - slightly different from background
  text: "#hex"         # Primary text color - high contrast with background
  text_muted: "#hex"   # Secondary text color - lower contrast for supporting text

# Color Usage Guidelines:
# - Ensure WCAG AA contrast ratios (4.5:1) between text and background colors
# - Primary should be distinctive and memorable
# - Secondary complements primary without competing
# - Accent should pop for calls-to-action
# - Surface provides subtle layering over background

---
# TYPOGRAPHY
# Font selection and hierarchy settings

typography:
  heading_font: "Font Name"  # REQUIRED: Google Fonts name or system font
    # Examples: "Inter", "Poppins", "SF Pro Display", "system-ui"
  body_font: "Font Name"     # REQUIRED: Google Fonts name or system font  
    # Examples: "Inter", "Source Sans Pro", "SF Pro Text", "system-ui"
  mono_font: "Font Name"     # OPTIONAL: For code blocks, technical content
    # Examples: "JetBrains Mono", "Fira Code", "SF Mono", "monospace"

  scale: tight | normal | loose  # REQUIRED: Overall typographic spacing
    # tight: Compact spacing, dense layouts (1.2-1.4 line height)
    # normal: Balanced spacing, most use cases (1.4-1.6 line height)  
    # loose: Airy spacing, premium feel (1.6-1.8 line height)

  heading_weight: 400-900    # REQUIRED: Font weight for headings (400, 500, 600, 700, etc.)

---
# LAYOUT SETTINGS
# Overall page structure and spacing

layout:
  max_width: narrow | standard | wide | full  # REQUIRED: Content container width
    # narrow: 768px - single column, blog-style content
    # standard: 1024px - balanced for most use cases
    # wide: 1280px - dashboard-style, more horizontal content
    # full: 100% - edge-to-edge, hero sections

  spacing: compact | balanced | airy  # REQUIRED: Section spacing rhythm
    # compact: 1-2rem between sections, tight layouts
    # balanced: 3-4rem between sections, most use cases
    # airy: 5-6rem between sections, premium/luxury feel

  border_radius: none | subtle | rounded | pill  # REQUIRED: Corner rounding style
    # none: 0 - sharp, technical, modern
    # subtle: 4px - minimal softness
    # rounded: 8-12px - friendly, approachable
    # pill: 9999px - fully rounded buttons/elements

---
# PAGE SECTIONS
# Ordered list of sections that make up the page structure.
# This defines the template's information architecture.

sections:  # REQUIRED: Array of section objects
  - type: hero           # REQUIRED: Section type (see valid types below)
    variant: default     # OPTIONAL: Specific variant of the section type
  
  - type: features
    variant: grid-3col
  
  - type: testimonials  
    variant: carousel
  
  - type: pricing
    variant: cards
  
  - type: cta
    variant: centered

# Valid Section Types:
# - hero: Main banner/header section with headline and CTA
# - features: Product/service features showcase  
# - testimonials: Customer testimonials and reviews
# - pricing: Pricing tables or plans
# - cta: Call-to-action sections
# - faq: Frequently asked questions
# - stats: Numbers, metrics, achievements
# - team: Team member profiles
# - logos: Client/partner logo grids
# - content: Generic content blocks

# Common Variants (by type):
# hero: default, centered, split, minimal, bold
# features: grid-2col, grid-3col, grid-4col, list, alternating
# testimonials: grid, carousel, single, wall
# pricing: cards, table, simple, comparison
# cta: centered, banner, inline, bottom

---
# STYLE NOTES
# Freeform description to capture nuances that structured fields can't express.
# This is critical for maintaining design integrity.

style_notes: |  # REQUIRED: Multi-line text
  Describe the overall aesthetic feel and distinctive characteristics:
  
  - Visual style (minimal, bold, playful, corporate, etc.)
  - Shadow usage (subtle, pronounced, none)
  - Border treatments (clean lines, rounded, none)
  - Gradient usage (subtle, bold, none)
  - Animation/interaction style (smooth, snappy, minimal)
  - Unique design elements that define this template
  - Content density preferences (spacious, balanced, compact)
  - Any specific design patterns or techniques used
  
  Examples:
  "Clean, minimal aesthetic with subtle shadows and plenty of whitespace. 
   Uses gradient overlays on hero images. Rounded corners throughout.
   Emphasizes horizontal layouts and grid-based organization."

---
# REFERENCE IMAGES (Optional)
# Visual references to help maintain design consistency

reference_images:  # OPTIONAL: Array of image references
  - path: /path/to/reference1.png    # Relative path to image file
    description: Screenshot of the original inspiration page
  
  - path: /path/to/reference2.png
    description: Color palette and typography samples

---
# CUSTOM FIELDS (Optional)
# Template-specific overrides and extensions for unique requirements

custom:  # OPTIONAL: Flexible key-value pairs
  # Add any template-specific configuration that doesn't fit standard schema
  # Examples:
  # animation_style: "smooth"
  # content_density: "high" 
  # interaction_pattern: "hover-lift"
  # special_sections: ["newsletter-signup", "social-proof"]
  
# Usage Notes:
# - Keep custom fields minimal - prefer standard schema when possible
# - Use custom fields for genuinely unique template characteristics
# - Document custom field meanings in comments

---
# SCHEMA VALIDATION NOTES
# 
# Required Fields: name, description, source, created, palette (all 7 colors), 
#                 typography (heading_font, body_font, scale, heading_weight),
#                 layout (max_width, spacing, border_radius), sections, style_notes
#
# Optional Fields: mono_font, section variants, reference_images, custom
#
# Color Format: All colors must be valid hex values (#000000 format)
# Date Format: created must be YYYY-MM-DD
# Enum Values: Must match exactly (case-sensitive)
#
# This schema balances structure with flexibility - use style_notes and custom 
# fields to capture nuances that don't fit the structured fields.",{"kind":"file_ref","path":"/home/workspace/Skills/frontend/templates/_TEMPLATE.yaml","media_type":null,"label":null}],"tool_call_id":"toolu_01UYXHuKXHyc9xQvgFpUas1R","metadata":null,"timestamp":"2026-02-06T20:50:04.021599Z","part_kind":"tool-return"}],"timestamp":null,"instructions":"","kind":"request","run_id":null,"metadata":null}
